_doc_status: SKELETON written 2026-05-16. Items marked TBD_* must be filled in by JT before Task 5.2 live verification. Schema validates with these placeholders but live integration will 4xx until real values are populated. See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-15-reply-router-implementation.md Prerequisites table.
_sending_inboxes_doc: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for loop prevention. Update BEFORE connecting any new inbox to Smartlead. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-reply-router-design.md §9.1.

classification_actions._doc_schema: Per-classification routing. Tags are NOT configurable here — they are hard-coded in routing.py by design (always 'replied' + classification name + conditional 'low_confidence'/'ambiguous_contact_match'/'auto_created_from_reply'). Adding new tag logic requires a code change, not a config change. The 'unsubscribe' entry's auto_send and min_confidence values are present for schema consistency but are IGNORED at runtime — see §5.4 carve-out (unsubscribe always honors regardless of confidence).

unsubscribe._doc: Static template. CAN-SPAM: bypasses confidence gate. See §5.4.

interested._doc: WEEK 4-6 OPTIMIZATION: flip auto_send true after 20-30 clean reviews. See §5.3.

not_now._doc: Date extraction populates contract_end_date when parseable.

info_request._doc: WEEK 4-6 OPTIMIZATION: flip auto_send true after 20-30 clean reviews.

objection._doc: WEEK 4-6 OPTIMIZATION: flip auto_send true after 20-30 clean reviews.

business_context._doc_status: Shawn-supplied content. Many fields are TBD until JT confirms specific marketing copy with Shawn. Skeleton uses derivable values from CFS engagement memory + intent-signal-engine config. Booking link is intentionally a PLACEHOLDER sentinel; orchestrator detects 'PLACEHOLDER' substring (case-insensitive) and forces shadow_send for interested/info_request/objection until JT provisions the real GHL calendar URL.
business_context.service_area: Minneapolis-St. Paul metro, 45-minute radius from downtown Minneapolis or downtown St. Paul
services_offered: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — likely includes: commercial office cleaning (nightly/weekly), warehouse + logistics facility cleaning, day porter service, post-construction cleanup, floor care (strip/wax/burnish), carpet extraction, window cleaning (interior), restroom sanitation programs, medical office cleaning
value_props: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — common framings: specialized equipment (auto-scrubbers for hard-floor properties), local Twin Cities owner-operator (not a national chain), 50%+ margin discipline keeps quality high (won't race to the bottom on price), responsiveness within the 45-min metro radius, route-density pricing for clusters of properties
business_context.pricing_response: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn_EXACT_WORDING — must NEVER quote $ amounts. Suggested baseline: 'Pricing depends on square footage, frequency, and what kind of facility you're running — happy to put a number together after a quick 10-minute walkthrough so I can see the space.'
business_context.scheduling_info: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical answer: 'We schedule walkthroughs within the week, usually mornings or after-hours so we don't disrupt your team.'
business_context.sales_process: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical answer: '10-min discovery call → 30-min on-site walkthrough → written proposal within 48 hours → start date typically within 2 weeks of signing.'

common_objections._doc: Pre-written response templates the contextual responder may use as-is or adapt. Add one key per objection theme Shawn hears in the wild during the shadow-mode review window.
common_objections.already_have_vendor: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'Totally understood — most folks we walk through with already have someone. Worth a 10-minute call just so we're on your bench if anything changes? Plenty of property managers we work with had a long-term vendor before they switched.'
common_objections.send_pricing_first: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'Happy to put something together, but pricing for facility cleaning really hinges on a quick walkthrough — square footage and what's actually in the space drive everything. 10 minutes on-site lets me put a real number in front of you rather than a guess.'
common_objections.not_in_budget: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'No problem. If anything shifts in the next quarter, would it be useful if I sent a quick check-in note in 60 days? Won't pester.'
common_objections.too_busy_now: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'Hear you. When does the dust settle — end of Q3 or sooner? I'll put a note on my side to circle back then.'
common_objections.contract_locked_in: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'When does that contract come up for renewal? Worth a walkthrough 60-90 days ahead so you have a real alternative on the table.'

credential_mentions._doc: Credential blurbs the responder may include for trust-building. Confirm with Shawn which ones are accurate + which he wants surfaced in cold-reply context.
credential_mentions.issa: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — if applicable: 'ISSA-certified team' (International Sanitary Supply Association).
credential_mentions.insurance: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'Fully insured and bonded; happy to share a COI.'
credential_mentions.background_checks: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'All on-site staff background-checked and W-2 employees (not subcontracted).'
credential_mentions.local_owner_operator: TBD_confirm_with_Shawn — typical: 'Twin Cities owner-operator since [year]; not a franchise.'
